wines.name: Krug Grande Cuvee
wines.oak_regime: Vinified in small oak casks, then blended across more than 120 wines from over ten vintages

taste.summary: A multi-vintage blend of extraordinary breadth: expressive toast and brioche over stone fruit, an unctuous creamy palate and a crisp mouthwatering lemon finish.

pairings.why: The wine's creamy weight and saline lemon finish flatter raw shellfish.
Toast and pine-nut notes echo the roast while acidity lifts the cream sauce.
Nutty barley-sugar depth meets the cheese's crystalline savour.

history.summary: Joseph Krug founded the house in 1843 with the radical idea of building a non-vintage prestige blend every year from a vast library of reserve wines, rather than chasing a single vintage. Each Grande Cuvee release carries an ID code tracing its blend and disgorgement.

milestones.event: Joseph Krug founds the house in Reims
Krug introduces the ID code linking each Grande Cuvee bottle to its blend and disgorgement

wines.description: Krug's multi-vintage flagship, a blend of more than 120 wines across ten-plus years built for breadth and length.

wines.name: Krug Rose
wines.oak_regime: Oak-cask fermentation with a high proportion of skin-macerated red Pinot Noir

taste.summary: A vinous, spice-driven rose with greater colour and texture from a relatively high red-wine proportion, the fruit framed by Krug's signature toast.

pairings.why: The wine's spice and red-fruit grip stand up to seared fish.
Blood-orange lift cuts the fat; spice echoes the crust.
Wild-strawberry fruit and toast frame cured meats.

history.summary: Krug shocked the market in 1983 by releasing a rose, applying the same multi-vintage, oak-fermented philosophy as Grande Cuvee to a blend incorporating macerated red Pinot Noir.

milestones.event: First Krug Rose released, the house's first non-Grande-Cuvee multi-vintage cuvee

wines.description: Krug's vinous multi-vintage rose, oak-fermented and built on a high proportion of macerated red Pinot Noir.

wines.name: Krug Clos du Mesnil
wines.oak_regime: Oak-cask fermentation, single walled-vineyard Chardonnay aged long on lees

taste.summary: A single walled-vineyard Le Mesnil Blanc de Blancs of intense purity: bone-dry, dense and saline with crystalline brightness and Krug's intellectual austerity.

pairings.why: Bone-dry saline drive and chalk meet the brine of raw oysters head-on.
Crystalline acidity slices the butter; chalk mirrors the white fish.
Saline precision flatters delicate raw fish.

history.summary: Krug bottled the first vintage of its single 1.84-hectare walled Le Mesnil-sur-Oger vineyard in 1979, releasing it as a vintage-dated Blanc de Blancs that became one of the rarest wines in Champagne.

milestones.event: First vintage of the single-vineyard Clos du Mesnil

wines.description: Krug's single walled-vineyard Le Mesnil Blanc de Blancs, among the rarest and most precise wines in Champagne.

wines.name: Moet Imperial Brut
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel, no oak; reserve wines from the house library

taste.summary: The most widely poured Champagne in the world: a bright, fruit-led brut of green apple and citrus with a brioche edge and an approachable, lively palate.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity cuts the richness of the toasted ham and cheese.
Citrus brightness refreshes the creamy egg.
Lively bubbles scrub fried batter clean.

history.summary: Moet's flagship brut, long called Brut Imperial after the house's association with Napoleon, is built from over a hundred wines to deliver a consistent, fruit-forward house style at vast scale.

milestones.event: Brut Imperial introduced as the house signature cuvee

wines.description: Moet's globally ubiquitous fruit-led brut, built from more than a hundred wines for consistency at scale.

wines.name: Dom Perignon
wines.oak_regime: No oak; long lees ageing on a single declared vintage to first Plenitude

taste.summary: A single-vintage prestige cuvee of silky, sculpted precision: white peach and candied citrus over a smoky, saline mineral core that the house calls its first plenitude.

pairings.why: Silky stone fruit and saline minerality frame fine white fish.
The wine's creamy texture and lift mirror the airy souffle.
Smoke and brioche notes echo roasted poultry skin.

history.summary: First produced from the 1921 vintage and released in 1936, Dom Perignon is made only in years deemed worthy of a vintage, always as a blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, and released after long lees ageing.

milestones.event: First commercial release, the 1921 vintage
House reframes successive disgorgements as Plenitudes P1, P2 and P3

wines.description: Moet's single-vintage prestige cuvee, a Chardonnay-Pinot blend released only in declared years after long lees ageing.

wines.name: Dom Perignon Rose
wines.oak_regime: No oak; long lees ageing, red-wine addition for colour

taste.summary: A bold, powerful vintage rose led by black cherry and stone fruit over a deep, softly spicy core, combining flesh and richness with zingy drive.

pairings.why: Blood-orange and cherry notes meet the duck and its citrus glaze.
Spicy depth and structure frame a rich game pate.
Cherry fruit and acidity balance the oily fish.

history.summary: Dom Perignon's vintage rose debuted with the 1959 vintage, famously served at the 1971 wedding of the Shah of Iran, and is made only in declared years from a Pinot Noir-led blend.

milestones.event: First Dom Perignon Rose vintage produced

wines.description: Dom Perignon's powerful Pinot-led vintage rose, made only in declared years.

wines.name: Dom Perignon P2
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended lees ageing to the second plenitude, around sixteen years

taste.summary: Dom Perignon held to its second plenitude: a generous, plump wine of slate and crushed rock over apricot, honeysuckle and mint, with a stretched, energetic glide.

pairings.why: Extended-lees richness and minerality meet sweet lobster meat.
Tropical depth and energy meet the cheese's nutty crystals.
Slate and stone fruit frame roasted poultry.

history.summary: Launched in 2014, P2 (Plenitude Deux) is Dom Perignon disgorged after roughly sixteen years on lees, capturing what the house describes as a second peak of energy and expression.

milestones.event: Dom Perignon P2 line launched, beginning with the 1998 vintage

wines.description: Dom Perignon held to its second plenitude, disgorged after around sixteen years on lees.

wines.name: Louis Roederer Collection
wines.oak_regime: Part oak-aged reserves held in a perpetual reserve since 2012

taste.summary: Roederer's perpetual-reserve multi-vintage brut: orchard fruit, chamomile and crushed rocks over brisk acids, the palate showing mature dried fruit, yeastiness and a savoury umami style.

pairings.why: Saline chalk and tension scrub the brine of raw oysters.
Creamy depth and citrus refresh the egg.
Chalky minerality lifts fresh cheese.

history.summary: In 2021 Roederer replaced its Brut Premier with Collection, built around a perpetual reserve begun with the 2012 harvest, giving the non-vintage a deeper reserve component.

milestones.event: Collection replaces Brut Premier, built on the 2012-founded perpetual reserve

wines.description: Roederer's perpetual-reserve multi-vintage brut, successor to Brut Premier.

wines.name: Louis Roederer Cristal
wines.oak_regime: Largely no malolactic; partial oak; estate, partly biodynamic fruit

taste.summary: The original prestige cuvee, created for a tsar: zesty candied lemon and pollen over roasted hazelnut and vanilla, with incredible tension and a chalky, honed precision.

pairings.why: Racy chalk and citrus precision flatter sweet shellfish.
Honed minerality and concentration frame fine white fish.
Saline tension and chalk meet the salt and richness of caviar.

history.summary: Created in 1876 for Tsar Alexander II in a clear lead-crystal bottle, Cristal is made only from estate vineyards, much of them now farmed biodynamically, and only in vintages judged worthy.

milestones.event: First Cristal created for Tsar Alexander II of Russia
House confirms Cristal sourced entirely from estate, largely biodynamic vineyards

wines.description: The first prestige cuvee, created for a Russian tsar in 1876, now made from estate, largely biodynamic fruit.

wines.name: Louis Roederer Cristal Rose
wines.oak_regime: Maceration; partial oak; estate, partly biodynamic fruit

taste.summary: Cristal in rose form: fine red fruit and pink grapefruit over chalk and spice, with a silky texture and the same saline, racy length as the white.

pairings.why: Red-fruit lift and chalk balance the oily fish.
Silky red fruit and spice meet the duck.
Saline chalk frames sweet shellfish meat.

history.summary: Roederer added a rose version of Cristal in 1974, built on the same estate, partly biodynamic Pinot Noir and Chardonnay with a portion macerated for colour.

milestones.event: First Cristal Rose vintage produced

wines.description: The rose version of Cristal, from estate, partly biodynamic fruit with a portion macerated for colour.

wines.name: Bollinger Special Cuvee Brut
wines.oak_regime: Fermented partly in old oak barrels; reserve wines stored in magnum under cork

taste.summary: A Pinot-led, oak-influenced brut of real depth: ripe apple and peach burst through with bitter orange while brioche and fresh walnut linger on a savoury finish.

pairings.why: The wine's weight and walnut notes meet roasted poultry.
Smoky depth and structure frame a rich pate.
Oak-driven richness and walnut echo the aged cheese.

history.summary: Bollinger registered the Special Cuvee name in 1911 to describe its drier house style, built on Pinot Noir, oak fermentation and reserve wines aged in magnum under cork.

milestones.event: Special Cuvee name registered for the dry house brut

wines.description: Bollinger's Pinot-led, oak-influenced house brut, with reserves aged in magnum under cork.

wines.name: Bollinger Rose
wines.oak_regime: Special Cuvee base with red wine from the Cote aux Enfants; partial oak

taste.summary: A structured Pinot-led rose built on the Special Cuvee base with red wine from Ay's Cote aux Enfants: raspberry and spice over smoky, savoury depth.

pairings.why: Red fruit and spice frame cured meats.
Smoky depth and structure meet the duck's fat.
Raspberry lift balances the oily fish.

history.summary: Bollinger launched its rose only in 2008, late by big-house standards, building it on the Special Cuvee blend with red wine from the historic Cote aux Enfants vineyard in Ay.

milestones.event: Bollinger Rose introduced, the house's first non-vintage rose

wines.description: Bollinger's structured Pinot-led rose, built on the Special Cuvee base with Ay red wine.

wines.name: Bollinger La Grande Annee
wines.oak_regime: Entirely barrel-fermented in old oak; long lees ageing under cork

taste.summary: Bollinger's barrel-fermented vintage cuvee: a marine nose of sea breeze, oyster shell and gunpowder smoke over hazelnut, with orchard fruit and fine vanilla on a savoury palate.

pairings.why: Marine, iodine notes meet sweet shellfish.
Smoky, savoury depth frames game birds.
Hazelnut and vanilla echo the cheese's depth.

history.summary: Bollinger's prestige vintage wine, fully fermented in old oak casks, takes its name from the great years it is made in; the 1921 is regarded as an early benchmark.

milestones.event: Early benchmark vintage of the barrel-fermented prestige wine
La Grande Annee name standardised for the vintage cuvee

wines.description: Bollinger's fully barrel-fermented vintage prestige cuvee, named for the great years it is made in.

wines.name: Bollinger R.D.
wines.oak_regime: Barrel-fermented; recently disgorged after extra-long lees ageing

taste.summary: Recently disgorged La Grande Annee held far longer on lees: oak and toasted coconut over dried apricot and mandarin, bone-dry yet fruit-packed with a very long finish.

pairings.why: Oak and toast meet the cheese's nutty crystals.
Bone-dry length and citrus frame fine white fish.
Tropical depth and aniseed echo mushroom and roast.

history.summary: Bollinger introduced R.D. (recemment degorge, recently disgorged) in 1967 with the 1952 vintage, releasing a Grande Annee kept on lees far longer and disgorged just before sale.

milestones.event: R.D. concept launched with the 1952 vintage

wines.description: Bollinger's recently disgorged prestige wine, a Grande Annee held far longer on lees.

wines.name: Pol Roger Brut Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; deep cellars and long ageing for a non-vintage

taste.summary: Pol Roger's white-foil house brut: mandarin oil, honeycomb and elderflower over fresh bread, the palate fleshy and enveloping with yellow apple, quince and apricot tart.

pairings.why: Bright citrus and bread notes refresh the egg.
Honeyed fruit and lift meet roasted poultry.
Acidity scrubs the rich ham and cheese.

history.summary: Pol Roger's non-vintage brut, the White Foil, was the favourite Champagne of Winston Churchill and is aged in the house's deep, cold Epernay cellars longer than most non-vintages.

milestones.event: Pol Roger founds the Epernay house behind the White Foil brut

wines.description: Pol Roger's White Foil house brut, Churchill's favourite, aged long in deep Epernay cellars.

wines.name: Pol Roger Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Cote des Blancs Chardonnay, long lees ageing

taste.summary: A vintage Blanc de Blancs of crisp golden orchard fruit and white flowers, full-bodied and broad with a fleshy core, lively acids and a precise chalky finish.

pairings.why: Chalky precision meets the brine of raw oysters.
Lively acid and chalk frame delicate white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Pol Roger's vintage Chardonnay-only cuvee draws on Cote des Blancs fruit and is released in declared years after extended lees ageing.

milestones.event: Vintage Blanc de Blancs established in the Pol Roger range

wines.description: Pol Roger's vintage Chardonnay-only cuvee from Cote des Blancs fruit.

wines.name: Pol Roger Cuvee Sir Winston Churchill
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Pinot-dominant prestige cuvee, long lees ageing

taste.summary: Pol Roger's Pinot-dominant prestige cuvee honouring Churchill: orange oil and confit lemon over brioche and honey, with bright saline cutting a core of apricot, mint and sage.

pairings.why: Saline drive and citrus meet sweet shellfish.
Brioche and honey echo roasted poultry.
Confit-lemon depth meets the nutty cheese.

history.summary: First made from the 1975 vintage and released in 1984, this prestige cuvee was created with the consent of the Churchill family to reflect the robust, Pinot-led style Sir Winston favoured.

milestones.event: First vintage of Cuvee Sir Winston Churchill produced
First commercial release of the prestige cuvee

wines.description: Pol Roger's Pinot-dominant prestige cuvee, created to reflect the style Churchill favoured.

wines.name: Veuve Clicquot Yellow Label Brut
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; high Pinot Noir proportion and substantial reserve wines

taste.summary: The famous yellow-label brut: a soft, youthful, fruity nose of vanilla and red apple with a toasty overtone, the palate fresh, rounded and approachable.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity cuts the toasted ham and cheese.
Bubbles and fruit scrub fried crust clean.
Rounded fruit refreshes the creamy egg.

history.summary: Veuve Clicquot registered its distinctive yellow label in 1877; the Pinot-led brut has been the house signature, built on a high proportion of reserve wines for consistency.

milestones.event: Distinctive yellow label registered as a trademark

wines.description: Veuve Clicquot's famous Pinot-led yellow-label brut, built on a high proportion of reserve wines.

wines.name: Veuve Clicquot Brut Rose
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Yellow Label base with added still red wine

taste.summary: A richly aromatic rose combining cherry, strawberry and raspberry with white pepper, saffron and biscuit, finished by toast, orange peel and gingerbread accents.

pairings.why: Red fruit and spice frame cured meats.
Cherry and citrus lift balance the oily fish.
Spice and gingerbread meet the rich duck.

history.summary: In 1818 Madame Clicquot blended the first known rose Champagne by adding still red wine, a method the house still uses for its Brut Rose.

milestones.event: Madame Clicquot creates the first known blended rose Champagne

wines.description: Veuve Clicquot's brut rose, made by blending still red wine into the Yellow Label base.

wines.name: Veuve Clicquot La Grande Dame
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Pinot-led prestige cuvee from historic Grand Cru plots

taste.summary: The house prestige cuvee named for Madame Clicquot: dried fig, apricot and ripe pear over hazelnut, the palate richly balanced with yellow plum and tropical lift on a fresh finish.

pairings.why: Rich balance and minerality meet sweet shellfish.
Hazelnut and stone fruit echo roasted poultry.
Tropical depth meets the cheese's nutty crystals.

history.summary: Created with the 1962 vintage and named La Grande Dame in 1972 for Madame Clicquot, this prestige cuvee draws on the house's historic Grand Cru holdings.

milestones.event: Prestige cuvee named La Grande Dame in honour of Madame Clicquot

wines.description: Veuve Clicquot's prestige cuvee, named for Madame Clicquot and drawn from historic Grand Cru plots.

wines.name: Veuve Clicquot La Grande Dame Rose
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; prestige cuvee with red wine from Bouzy

taste.summary: The rose version of the prestige cuvee: powerful red fruit and citrus over rooibos tea and fine leather, the palate complex with dried flowers and smoke on a luscious finish.

pairings.why: Red-fruit power and leather meet the duck.
Citrus and red fruit balance the oily fish.
Smoke and dried flowers frame a rich pate.

history.summary: Veuve Clicquot added a rose version of La Grande Dame in 1988, building it on the same Grand Cru base with red wine from Bouzy.

milestones.event: First La Grande Dame Rose vintage produced

wines.description: The rose version of La Grande Dame, built on a Grand Cru base with red wine from Bouzy.

wines.name: Taittinger Brut Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Chardonnay-leaning house blend with reserve wines

taste.summary: Taittinger's Chardonnay-leaning house brut: white peach and apple blossom over fresh fruit and brioche, the palate light, fresh and elegant.

pairings.why: Chardonnay freshness and lift meet the brine of oysters.
Citrus and pear balance fresh cheese.
Light, fresh palate refreshes the egg.

history.summary: Taittinger's non-vintage brut reflects the house's Chardonnay leaning, drawing on Cote des Blancs sourcing for a fresh, refined style.

milestones.event: House traces its origins to the Forest-Fourneaux maison

wines.description: Taittinger's Chardonnay-leaning house brut, drawing on Cote des Blancs sourcing.

wines.name: Taittinger Prestige Rose
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; brut blend with around 15 percent still red wine

taste.summary: A delicate, perfumed rose with a bouquet of strawberry, red berries, herbs, blood orange and guava, fresh and lively on the palate.

pairings.why: Red fruit and herbs frame cured meats.
Lively red fruit meets seared fish.
Strawberry notes echo the dessert fruit.

history.summary: Taittinger's non-vintage rose is built by blending around fifteen percent still red wine into a Pinot-led base, giving its perfumed red-fruit profile.

milestones.event: Taittinger establishes its non-vintage Prestige Rose

wines.description: Taittinger's perfumed non-vintage rose, built with around fifteen percent still red wine.

wines.name: Taittinger Comtes de Champagne Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Around 5 percent aged in oak; Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay

taste.summary: One of the great vintage Blanc de Blancs, made only from Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay: citrus and white flowers over chalk, with creamy texture and saline length.

pairings.why: Racy chalk and citrus meet the brine of oysters.
Saline length and minerality frame fine white fish.
Creamy texture mirrors the airy souffle.

history.summary: First produced in 1952, Comtes de Champagne is a Grand Cru-only Blanc de Blancs from the Cote des Blancs, made just in the best vintages and a benchmark for the style.

milestones.event: First vintage of Comtes de Champagne Blanc de Blancs

wines.description: Taittinger's Grand Cru-only vintage Blanc de Blancs, a benchmark of the style.

wines.name: Taittinger Comtes de Champagne Rose
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; prestige rose with Bouzy red wine

taste.summary: The prestige rose counterpart to Comtes: red berries and blood orange over rose and spice, the palate silky with raspberry, chalk and a long finish.

pairings.why: Silky red fruit and spice meet the duck.
Blood-orange lift balances the oily fish.
Raspberry and spice frame the rich terrine.

history.summary: Taittinger's vintage prestige rose, made from a Pinot-led blend with Bouzy red wine, sits alongside the Blanc de Blancs as the other half of Comtes de Champagne.

milestones.event: First Comtes de Champagne Rose vintage produced

wines.description: Taittinger's vintage prestige rose, a Pinot-led blend with Bouzy red wine.

wines.name: Ruinart Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; multi-vintage Chardonnay, signature house style

taste.summary: Ruinart's signature multi-vintage Chardonnay: white flowers and citrus over a chalky, creamy palate with a fresh finish.

pairings.why: Chalk and citrus meet the brine of oysters.
Creamy texture and lemon frame white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Ruinart, the oldest established Champagne house, built its modern identity on Chardonnay; its non-vintage Blanc de Blancs is the house calling card.

milestones.event: Ruinart established as the first Champagne house

wines.description: Ruinart's signature multi-vintage Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs, the house calling card.

wines.name: R de Ruinart Brut
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Pinot-led house brut

taste.summary: Ruinart's Pinot-led house brut: yellow fruit and citrus over brioche and white flowers, the palate rounded and fresh.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity cuts the toasted ham and cheese.
Yellow fruit and brioche meet roasted poultry.
Rounded fruit frames cured meats.

history.summary: R de Ruinart is the house's Pinot-led non-vintage brut, a counterpoint to its Chardonnay-driven Blanc de Blancs.

milestones.event: House founded; the brut later codified as R de Ruinart

wines.description: Ruinart's Pinot-led house brut, a counterpoint to its Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs.

wines.name: Ruinart Rose
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; brut with red wine, an early historic rose

taste.summary: An early historic rose, fresh and delicate: red berries and rose over citrus, the palate marked by raspberry and redcurrant with a chalky finish.

pairings.why: Red-fruit lift balances the oily fish.
Raspberry and spice frame cured meats.
Red-berry notes echo the dessert fruit.

history.summary: Ruinart was making rose Champagne as early as 1764, among the first houses to do so; the modern Ruinart Rose continues that tradition.

milestones.event: Ruinart records one of the earliest rose Champagnes

wines.description: Ruinart's rose, continuing a tradition dating to one of the earliest roses in Champagne.

wines.name: Dom Ruinart Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Reductive winemaking; Grand Cru Chardonnay, long lees ageing

taste.summary: Ruinart's prestige cuvee, a Grand Cru-only pure-Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs in a firm, muscular, reductive style with chalk and long, lingering flavour.

pairings.why: Reductive chalk and citrus meet the brine of oysters.
Muscular structure and minerality frame fine white fish.
Chestnut and bread notes meet the nutty cheese.

history.summary: Dom Ruinart, named for the Benedictine monk Thierry Ruinart, debuted with the 1959 vintage as a Grand Cru Chardonnay prestige cuvee aged long in the house's deep chalk crayeres.

milestones.event: First Dom Ruinart Blanc de Blancs vintage produced

wines.description: Ruinart's Grand Cru-only pure-Chardonnay prestige cuvee, aged long in deep chalk crayeres.

wines.name: Dom Ruinart Rose
wines.oak_regime: Reductive winemaking; Grand Cru base with red wine, long lees ageing

taste.summary: A rare Blanc de Blancs-based prestige rose: copper-hued and complex with mature Pinot, apricot and spice over gunpowder toast, vanilla and layered citrus.

pairings.why: Spice and mature Pinot notes meet the duck.
Toast and citrus frame sweet shellfish.
Apricot and spice frame the rich terrine.

history.summary: Dom Ruinart Rose, made from a Chardonnay-dominant base with red wine, is among the few prestige roses built on a Blanc de Blancs heart.

milestones.event: First Dom Ruinart Rose vintage produced

wines.description: Ruinart's rare prestige rose, built on a Chardonnay-dominant Blanc de Blancs base.

wines.name: Billecart-Salmon Brut Rose
wines.oak_regime: Cold, slow double fermentation; small still-red addition for colour

taste.summary: One of Champagne's most admired roses: pale salmon-pink with wild strawberry, redcurrant and white peach over fine red fruit, saline freshness and a silky, precise finish.

pairings.why: Saline freshness and red fruit meet raw fish.
Delicate spice frames cured meats.
Wild-strawberry notes echo the dessert fruit.

history.summary: Billecart-Salmon's brut rose, made with the house's signature cold, slow fermentation, is one of the most acclaimed non-vintage roses in Champagne.

milestones.event: House establishes the cold-fermentation method behind its rose

wines.description: Billecart-Salmon's acclaimed pale-pink brut rose, made by cold, slow fermentation.

wines.name: Billecart-Salmon Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru
wines.oak_regime: Cold, slow fermentation; Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay

taste.summary: A Grand Cru Chardonnay of cold-fermented precision: white flowers and lemon over chalk, with creamy texture cut by saline tension and a precise finish.

pairings.why: Saline chalk and citrus meet the brine of oysters.
Racy tension and minerality frame delicate white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Billecart-Salmon's Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs draws on Cote des Blancs Chardonnay, vinified with the cold, slow fermentation that defines the house.

milestones.event: House founded at Mareuil-sur-Ay by the Billecart and Salmon families

wines.description: Billecart-Salmon's Grand Cru Chardonnay, vinified with the house's cold, slow fermentation.

wines.name: Billecart-Salmon Cuvee Nicolas Francois
wines.oak_regime: Cold fermentation; long lees ageing; Pinot-led prestige cuvee

taste.summary: The house prestige cuvee: fresh bread and citrus oil over yellow orchard fruit, verbena and macadamia, full-bodied and chiseled with a vinous core cloaking brisk acids.

pairings.why: Fleshy vinous fruit and acidity meet sweet shellfish.
Bread and macadamia notes echo roasted poultry.
Citrus-oil depth meets the nutty cheese.

history.summary: Named for the house founder, Cuvee Nicolas Francois Billecart debuted with the 1959 vintage and was first released in 1964 as the maison prestige bottling.

milestones.event: First release of Cuvee Nicolas Francois Billecart, the 1959 vintage

wines.description: Billecart-Salmon's prestige cuvee, named for the house founder and first released in 1964.

wines.name: Billecart-Salmon Cuvee Louis Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Cold fermentation; Grand Cru Chardonnay, long lees ageing

taste.summary: A vintage Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs prestige cuvee: floral notes with hazelnut and chestnut bread over a creamy, full-bodied palate of baked goods, mango and candied chestnut.

pairings.why: Racy chalk and citrus meet the brine of oysters.
Creamy finesse and minerality frame fine white fish.
Hazelnut and chestnut notes meet the nutty cheese.

history.summary: Cuvee Louis is Billecart-Salmon's vintage Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs, drawing on Chardonnay from the Cote des Blancs for a more powerful prestige expression.

milestones.event: Cuvee Louis Blanc de Blancs established as the vintage prestige Chardonnay

wines.description: Billecart-Salmon's vintage Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs prestige cuvee.

wines.name: Charles Heidsieck Brut Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Touch of oak; very high proportion of mature reserve wines

taste.summary: A richly mature non-vintage built on a large reserve component: ripe lemon and pineapple over buttery pastry and vanilla toast, with textural richness lifted by a saline, chalky finish.

pairings.why: Buttery pastry and toast meet roasted poultry.
Textural richness and vanilla echo the aged cheese.
Saline lift refreshes the creamy egg.

history.summary: Charles-Camille Heidsieck founded the house in 1851; its Brut Reserve is built on an unusually large proportion of mature reserve wines for a rich, toasty style.

milestones.event: Charles-Camille Heidsieck founds the house in Reims

wines.description: Charles Heidsieck's richly mature non-vintage, built on a large reserve component.

wines.name: Charles Heidsieck Blanc des Millenaires
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Grand and Premier Cru Chardonnay, long lees ageing

taste.summary: A toasty, tropical vintage Blanc de Blancs: candied lemon and baked pineapple with coffee and floral tones, the round palate cut by minerality and high acidity on a long, pure, dry finish.

pairings.why: Round richness and minerality meet sweet shellfish.
High acidity and toast frame fine white fish.
Coffee and candied-lemon depth meet the cheese.

history.summary: Les Pierrieres is Olivier Collin's single-vineyard Blanc de Blancs from chalky Sezannais soils, oak-fermented in a textural, low-dosage style.

milestones.event: Collin bottles his first single-vineyard cuvees

wines.description: Ulysse Collin's single-vineyard Sezannais Blanc de Blancs, oak-fermented and textural.

wines.name: Roses de Jeanne Cote de Val Vilaine
wines.oak_regime: No dosage; single parcel; single vintage; Cote des Bar

taste.summary: A single-parcel, no-dosage Blanc de Noirs from the Cote des Bar: pear, green apple and bread dough over almond paste and white flowers, pillowy and precise with a mineral imprint.

pairings.why: Pillowy precise fruit meets roasted poultry.
Red-fruit lift frames cured meats.
Mineral imprint echoes earthy mushrooms.

history.summary: Cote de Val Vilaine is Cedric Bouchard's single-parcel Pinot Noir from Polisot, bottled as a single grape, single vintage and single parcel with no dosage.

milestones.event: Bouchard begins bottling single-parcel Roses de Jeanne cuvees

wines.description: Cedric Bouchard's single-parcel, no-dosage Cote des Bar Blanc de Noirs.

wines.name: Drappier Carte d'Or Brut
wines.oak_regime: Low-sulfite winemaking; Cote des Bar Pinot Noir-led; low dosage

taste.summary: Drappier's Pinot Noir-led Cote des Bar house brut: quince, red apple and spice over fresh brioche, with ripe Pinot fruit, a rounded texture and a long finish.

pairings.why: Ripe Pinot fruit meets roasted poultry.
Spice and red fruit frame cured meats.
Rounded richness echoes the aged cheese.

history.summary: Drappier's flagship Carte d'Or is a Pinot Noir-led brut from the Cote des Bar at Urville, made with the family's low-sulfite, low-dosage approach.

milestones.event: Drappier family establishes the house at Urville

wines.description: Drappier's Pinot Noir-led Cote des Bar house brut, low-sulfite and low-dosage.

wines.name: Drappier Quattuor Blanc de Quatre Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Low-sulfite winemaking; four white grapes in equal parts

taste.summary: A rare four-white-grape Champagne in equal parts: acacia honey, beeswax and marzipan giving way to quince jelly, raspberry jam and warm brioche, textural and exotic with fresh acidity.

pairings.why: Exotic fruit and honey echo roasted poultry.
Honey and beeswax notes meet the rich foie gras.
Marzipan and texture meet the aged cheese.

history.summary: Quattuor is Drappier's custodial blend of the rare Arbane, Petit Meslier and Pinot Blanc with Chardonnay in equal parts, preserving Champagne's almost-forgotten white grapes.

milestones.event: Quattuor launched as a four-white-grape cuvee

wines.description: Drappier's rare four-white-grape Champagne, preserving Champagne's forgotten varieties.

wines.name: Gimonnet Cuis 1er Cru Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Premier Cru Cuis Chardonnay, fresh non-oaked style

taste.summary: A pure Premier Cru Cuis Blanc de Blancs: lemon and ripe apple over white flowers and light brioche, the palate bringing ripe citrus and yellow plum with underlying minerality and a crisp dry finish.

pairings.why: Crisp minerality meets the brine of oysters.
Ripe citrus and chalk frame delicate white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Gimonnet's Cuis Premier Cru is a fresh, non-oaked Blanc de Blancs from the family's extensive Cote des Blancs holdings around Cuis, Cramant and Chouilly.

milestones.event: Gimonnet begins estate bottling its Cote des Blancs Chardonnay

wines.description: Gimonnet's fresh, non-oaked Premier Cru Cuis Blanc de Blancs from the Cote des Blancs.

wines.name: Savart L'Ouverture
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and old oak; Premier Cru Ecueil pinot fin; low dosage

taste.summary: Frederic Savart's Premier Cru Ecueil Blanc de Noirs: red apple and red berries over white flowers and spice, fresh and vinous with a fine chalk and saline cut.

pairings.why: Fresh vinous Pinot fruit meets roasted poultry.
Red fruit and spice frame cured meats.
Chalk and citrus balance fresh cheese.

history.summary: L'Ouverture is Frederic Savart's signature Premier Cru Ecueil Blanc de Noirs, built on the prized pinot fin d'Ecueil, an old massale selection of Pinot Noir.

milestones.event: Savart begins estate bottling at Ecueil

wines.description: Frederic Savart's Premier Cru Ecueil Blanc de Noirs, built on old pinot fin d'Ecueil.

wines.name: Marguet Shaman Grand Cru
wines.oak_regime: Part oak; biodynamic Grand Cru Ambonnay and Bouzy; low dosage

taste.summary: Benoit Marguet's biodynamic Grand Cru blend: citrus and orchard fruit over brioche and nutty notes, with a delicious briochy softness and enchanting oxidative nuttiness.

pairings.why: Briochy softness meets roasted poultry.
Oxidative nuttiness echoes the aged cheese.
Savoury depth frames cured meats.

history.summary: Shaman is Marguet's Grand Cru entry cuvee from biodynamically farmed Ambonnay and Bouzy, the estate ploughed with horses since 2010 and Demeter-certified.

milestones.event: Marguet adopts horse ploughing and biodynamics for Shaman

wines.description: Marguet's biodynamic Grand Cru entry cuvee from Ambonnay and Bouzy.

wines.name: Bereche Brut Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Part oak; low dosage; extended lees ageing; reserve wines

taste.summary: Bereche's terroir-driven non-vintage: citrus and orchard fruit over white flowers and subtle toast, with fine chalk, saline drive and a vinous depth from low dosage and extended ageing.

pairings.why: Saline chalk drive meets the brine of oysters.
Vinous depth meets roasted poultry.
Citrus and chalk balance fresh cheese.

history.summary: The Brut Reserve is Bereche's low-dosage, extended-aged non-vintage, the wine that helped build the Ludes estate's modern reputation under brothers Raphael and Vincent.

milestones.event: Bereche family establishes the estate at Ludes

wines.description: Bereche's terroir-driven, low-dosage non-vintage, the wine behind the estate's modern reputation.

wines.name: Paul Bara Brut Reserve Grand Cru
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; 100 percent Grand Cru Bouzy; reserve wines

taste.summary: A 100 percent Grand Cru Bouzy brut: ripe red fruit and citrus over white flowers and brioche, with rich classy fruit, concentrated Pinot flavour and excellent acidity that gains biscuity complexity with age.

pairings.why: Concentrated Pinot fruit meets roasted poultry.
Rich red fruit frames cured meats.
Biscuity depth echoes the aged cheese.

history.summary: Paul Bara's Brut Reserve comes entirely from Grand Cru Bouzy, famed for ripe, powerful Pinot Noir, from a seventh-generation grower family.

milestones.event: Bara family begins growing in Grand Cru Bouzy

wines.description: Paul Bara's Grand Cru Bouzy brut, all from ripe, powerful Pinot Noir.

wines.name: Paul Bara Grand Rose de Bouzy
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Grand Cru Bouzy with red wine; Pinot Noir only

taste.summary: A textbook Bouzy rose from Pinot Noir alone: floral strawberry and red berries over rose and spice, with a deep Pinot character and fresh acidity.

pairings.why: Deep Pinot fruit frames cured meats.
Spice and red fruit meet the duck.
Strawberry notes echo the dessert fruit.

history.summary: Paul Bara's renowned Grand Rose comes from Grand Cru Bouzy, long famed for the ripe Pinot Noir that gives one of Champagne's classic roses.

milestones.event: Bara family establishes its Grand Cru Bouzy holdings

wines.description: Paul Bara's classic Grand Cru Bouzy rose, from Pinot Noir alone.

wines.name: Vazart-Coquart Grand Bouquet Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Grand Cru Chouilly Chardonnay; large perpetual reserve

taste.summary: A Grand Cru Chouilly Blanc de Blancs: fresh lemon and hazelnut over white flowers and vanilla, with a smooth mousse, zesty lemon and a chalky character through a long citrus finish.

pairings.why: Chalky lemon zest meets the brine of oysters.
Zesty citrus and chalk frame white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Grand Bouquet is Vazart-Coquart's vintage Grand Cru Chouilly Blanc de Blancs, drawing on the estate's large perpetual reserve.

milestones.event: Vazart-Coquart begins estate bottling at Chouilly

wines.description: Vazart-Coquart's vintage Grand Cru Chouilly Blanc de Blancs from a large perpetual reserve.

wines.name: Vazart-Coquart Special Club Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Grand Cru Chouilly old-vine Chardonnay; Special Club bottle

taste.summary: A Grand Cru Chouilly Special Club Blanc de Blancs: lemon and lime with heady lily and sweet vanilla, very fresh with a beautiful chalky character and a saline cut.

pairings.why: Saline chalk meets the brine of oysters.
Racy citrus and chalk frame fine white fish.
Lemon-lime drive meets the nutty cheese.

history.summary: Special Club is Vazart-Coquart's prestige Grand Cru Chouilly Blanc de Blancs, bottled in the shared Club Tresors de Champagne flacon by member growers.

milestones.event: Club Tresors de Champagne Special Club bottling established

wines.description: Vazart-Coquart's prestige Grand Cru Chouilly Special Club Blanc de Blancs.

wines.name: Diebolt-Vallois Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Grand Cru Cote des Blancs (Cuis and Chouilly) Chardonnay

taste.summary: A vintage Cote des Blancs Blanc de Blancs from the northern villages: lemon and white flowers over almond and chalk, reflecting the year's easy charms with a fine chalky finish.

pairings.why: Fine chalk meets the brine of oysters.
Lemon and chalk frame delicate white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Diebolt-Vallois' Blanc de Blancs draws on Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay from Cuis and Chouilly for an open, charming vintage expression.

milestones.event: Diebolt-Vallois established as a Cramant Chardonnay grower

wines.description: Diebolt-Vallois' vintage Cote des Blancs Blanc de Blancs from Cuis and Chouilly.

wines.name: Diebolt-Vallois Fleur de Passion
wines.oak_regime: Old-oak fermentation; old-vine Grand Cru Cramant; no malolactic

taste.summary: A stately old-vine Cramant Blanc de Blancs fermented in old oak without malolactic: citrus and white flowers over toasted nuts and chalk, with creamy texture and saline mineral depth.

pairings.why: Saline chalk meets the brine of oysters.
Creamy depth and minerality frame fine white fish.
Toasted-nut depth meets the nutty cheese.

history.summary: Fleur de Passion is Diebolt-Vallois' prestige cuvee from old Cramant vines, fermented in old oak and made without malolactic fermentation.

milestones.event: Fleur de Passion established as the estate's old-vine prestige cuvee

wines.description: Diebolt-Vallois' stately old-vine Cramant prestige cuvee, oak-fermented without malolactic.

wines.name: Gaston Chiquet Tradition Brut 1er Cru
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Premier Cru Dizy three-grape house blend

taste.summary: Gaston Chiquet's Premier Cru house brut: playful and zesty with mandarin and lemon complementing red fruit, an affable, forward style with fresh acidity.

pairings.why: Fresh zesty acidity cuts the ham and cheese.
Red fruit and citrus meet roasted poultry.
Playful fruit refreshes the egg.

history.summary: Gaston Chiquet's Tradition is the family's Premier Cru Dizy house brut, bottled by one of Champagne's pioneering grower families since the 1930s.

milestones.event: Gaston Chiquet begins estate bottling at Dizy

wines.description: Gaston Chiquet's playful Premier Cru Dizy house brut.

wines.name: Gaston Chiquet Blanc de Blancs d'Ay
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; rare Chardonnay grown in Pinot-stronghold Ay

taste.summary: A rare Blanc de Blancs from Chardonnay grown in Ay, a village famed for Pinot Noir: citrus and white flowers over ripe orchard fruit and brioche, with a fuller-bodied Chardonnay character and fine chalk.

pairings.why: Fine chalk meets the brine of oysters.
Fuller Chardonnay fruit meets roasted poultry.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Gaston Chiquet's Blanc de Blancs d'Ay is a rarity: Chardonnay grown in Ay, a village far better known for the Pinot Noir behind houses like Bollinger.

milestones.event: Family bottles a rare Ay Chardonnay as a Blanc de Blancs

wines.description: Gaston Chiquet's rare Blanc de Blancs from Chardonnay grown in Pinot-famed Ay.

wines.name: La Closerie Les Beguines
wines.oak_regime: Old-oak fermentation; single-vineyard old-vine Pinot Meunier; no dosage

taste.summary: Jerome Prevost's cult single-vineyard Pinot Meunier from Gueux: brioche straight from the oven with spice and ripe orchard fruit, burnished and vinous with masterly oak, the most exotic Meunier in Champagne.

pairings.why: Vinous brioche richness meets roasted poultry.
Smoke and orchard fruit echo earthy mushrooms.
Spice and depth frame cured meats.

history.summary: Les Beguines is Jerome Prevost's cult cuvee built almost entirely on old-vine Pinot Meunier from a single parcel near Gueux; though labelled non-vintage, it is always the wine of a single year.

milestones.event: Prevost begins bottling Les Beguines from the single Gueux parcel

wines.description: Jerome Prevost's cult single-vineyard old-vine Pinot Meunier, the most exotic Meunier in Champagne.

wines.name: La Closerie Fac-Simile Rose
wines.oak_regime: Old-oak fermentation; single-vineyard Pinot Meunier with saignee red wine; no dosage

taste.summary: The rose counterpart to Les Beguines, from the same Gueux Pinot Meunier with a saignee addition: vinous red berries and spice over orchard fruit and smoke, savoury and deep with masterly oak.

pairings.why: Vinous red fruit and spice meet the duck.
Savoury depth frames cured meats.
Smoke echoes earthy mushrooms.

history.summary: Fac-Simile is Prevost's rose version of Les Beguines, made from the same single-vineyard old-vine Pinot Meunier with a small saignee addition.

milestones.event: Prevost adds the Fac-Simile rose to the range

wines.description: Prevost's rose version of Les Beguines, from the same single-vineyard old-vine Pinot Meunier.

wines.name: Gosset Grand Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Malolactic blocked; multi-vintage Chardonnay; non-malolactic house style

taste.summary: Gosset's non-vintage Chardonnay: white flowers and citrus over almond and brioche, with fresh non-malolactic acidity, chalk and a creamy texture.

pairings.why: Racy non-malolactic acidity meets the brine of oysters.
Chalk and citrus frame delicate white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Gosset's Grand Blanc de Blancs applies the house's non-malolactic style to a multi-vintage Chardonnay for a fresh, chalky expression.

milestones.event: House founded; Chardonnay cuvee made in its non-malolactic style

wines.description: Gosset's non-vintage Chardonnay, made in the house's fresh non-malolactic style.

wines.name: Egly-Ouriet Les Premices
wines.oak_regime: Long lees ageing; Premier Cru fruit; low dosage entry cuvee

taste.summary: Egly-Ouriet's Premier Cru entry cuvee: ripe orchard fruit and white flowers over spice and light toast, fleshy and vinous with fine chalk and a savoury depth.

pairings.why: Fleshy vinous fruit meets roasted poultry.
Spice and savoury depth frame cured meats.
Toast and chalk echo the aged cheese.

history.summary: Les Premices is Egly-Ouriet's more accessible Premier Cru entry cuvee, made in the estate's long-aged, low-dosage style.

milestones.event: Egly adds the Premier Cru Les Premices entry cuvee

wines.description: Egly-Ouriet's accessible Premier Cru entry cuvee in the estate's long-aged style.

wines.name: Gimonnet Fleuron 1er Cru Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; Premier and Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Chardonnay, vintage

taste.summary: Gimonnet's vintage Premier and Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs: lemon and ripe apple over white flowers and brioche, with ripe citrus, fine chalk and a long crisp finish.

pairings.why: Fine chalk meets the brine of oysters.
Ripe citrus and chalk frame white fish.
White-flower lift balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Fleuron is Gimonnet's vintage Cote des Blancs Blanc de Blancs, drawing on the family's Premier and Grand Cru holdings for a riper, more structured expression than the Cuis cuvee.

milestones.event: Gimonnet establishes its vintage Blanc de Blancs program

wines.description: Gimonnet's vintage Premier and Grand Cru Cote des Blancs Blanc de Blancs.

wines.name: Larmandier-Bernier Latitude Blanc de Blancs
wines.oak_regime: Part oak; extra-brut; biodynamic Premier Cru Vertus southern Cote des Blancs

taste.summary: A biodynamic Premier Cru Vertus extra-brut Blanc de Blancs from the southern Cote des Blancs: lemon and white peach over chalk and white flowers, with fine mineral drive and a saline, dry finish.

pairings.why: Saline mineral drive meets the brine of oysters.
Lemon and chalk frame delicate white fish.
Minerality balances fresh cheese.

history.summary: Latitude is Larmandier-Bernier's non-vintage extra-brut Blanc de Blancs from the warmer, southern Vertus parcels, farmed biodynamically and Demeter-certified.

milestones.event: Latitude established as a southern-Vertus non-vintage Blanc de Blancs

wines.description: Larmandier-Bernier's biodynamic southern-Vertus extra-brut Blanc de Blancs.

wines.name: Krug Vintage
wines.oak_regime: Oak-cask fermentation; single declared vintage; long lees ageing

taste.summary: Krug's expression of a single year: citrus and stone fruit over toasted nuts, spice and honey, with vinous depth, fine chalk and a complex, savoury, very long finish.

pairings.why: Vinous depth meets roast and mushroom.
Toasted-nut savour meets the cheese's crystals.
Chalk and depth meet sweet shellfish.

history.summary: Krug Vintage captures the character of a single year, made only when the house judges the harvest worthy and aged long in its oak-influenced style.

milestones.event: Early benchmark Krug Vintage release

wines.description: Krug's single-vintage cuvee, made only in worthy years in the house oak-influenced style.
